description: Discovery reduces risk before committing to delivery. These discovery tracks test whether a potential solution is valuable, viable, usable, and feasible.

riskFramework.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.title: Energy and charging offer packaging discovery
items.name: Energy and charging offer packaging discovery
items.summary: Discovery work in DKV is reducing uncertainty before committing to larger product and platform investment.
items.opportunity: Validated how bundled charging, fleet control, and pricing support should be packaged for mixed-energy customers.

assignments.how: Led the framing, evidence gathering, and recommendation for this discovery track.
Supported feasibility and operating-model validation across adjacent systems.
Supported feasibility and operating-model validation across adjacent systems.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Target customers will adopt a more unified workflow if it reduces fragmented mobility administration.
evidenceToCollect: Evidence of current workflow pain and switching intent.
Signals that the proposed experience changes purchase or adoption behavior.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The proposed offer can improve growth or control metrics without unacceptable operating cost.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers can understand the flow and complete the core jobs without specialist support.
evidenceToCollect: Workflow friction across key steps and handoffs.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The smallest useful slice fits current platform and compliance constraints.
evidenceToCollect: Dependencies on payment, risk, or platform teams.
Whether the solution can be delivered incrementally.

plannedActivities: Interview target customers and internal stakeholders on workflow pain and purchase criteria.
Prototype the critical workflow and test task completion.
Review architecture, compliance, and operating constraints before committing to delivery.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to proceed, narrow, or stop the opportunity before further investment.

outcome.summary: Discovery confirmed the opportunity but narrowed the first delivery slice to reduce execution risk.
